name: axon-copilot
description: Connect GitHub Copilot to your local Axon RAG knowledge base

chatParticipants.name: axon
chatParticipants.description: Ask questions answered by your local Axon RAG knowledge base. Uses the same backend synthesis as query_knowledge — consistent behavior, no separate Copilot step.

languageModelTools.name: search_knowledge
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the Axon knowledge base and return raw document chunks. Returns source text — you synthesize the answer. Also opens the knowledge graph panel. Use for discovery tasks or when inspecting multiple documents.

top_k.description: Number of chunks to return (default 5). Use 20+ for broad discovery.

threshold.description: Minimum similarity score 0.0-1.0 (default 0.3). Lower to 0.1 if search returns no results.

filters.description: Optional metadata filters, e.g. {"source": "https://..."}. Narrows results to chunks matching the filter.

project.description: Optional. Target project name. Omit to use the active project. Use switch_project to change projects first.

languageModelTools.name: query_knowledge
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Ask a question and get a synthesized answer from the Axon knowledge base. Use for direct questions. For browsing or discovery, use search_knowledge instead.

top_k.description: Number of chunks to retrieve for context (default uses global setting; increase for broad questions)

filters.description: Optional metadata filters for retrieval, e.g. {"source": "https://..."}.

project.description: Optional. Target project name. Omit to use the active project. Use switch_project to change projects first.

languageModelTools.name: ingest_text
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Ingest a raw text snippet into the Axon knowledge base.

project.description: Optional. Target project name. Omit to use the active project.

languageModelTools.name: ingest_url
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Fetch and ingest a web page into the Axon knowledge base.

project.description: Optional. Target project name. Omit to use the active project.

languageModelTools.name: ingest_path
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Ingest a local directory or file into the Axon knowledge base. Returns a job_id immediately  -- ingestion runs in the background. Always call axon_getIngestStatus with the returned job_id and wait until status is 'completed' before searching. Accepts any absolute path on the local machine (e.g. /home/user/docs or C:\Users\user\docs) or a path relative to the workspace root.

path.description: The local file or directory path to ingest

languageModelTools.name: get_job_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Poll the status of a background ingest job started by ingest_path. Returns 'processing', 'completed', or 'failed'. Always call this after ingest_path and wait for 'completed' before running searches.

languageModelTools.name: list_projects
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all knowledge base projects. Call first if search returns nothing — data may be in a different project. Then use switch_project.

languageModelTools.name: switch_project
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Switch the active Axon knowledge base project. All subsequent searches and queries will run against the new project. Use after list_projects discovers that relevant documents are in a non-default project.

name.description: The project name to switch to (e.g. 'engineering', 'research', 'default')

languageModelTools.name: create_project
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create a new knowledge base project in Axon.

name.description: The name of the new project

languageModelTools.name: delete_project
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Delete an existing Axon project and all its stored data.

name.description: The name of the project to delete

languageModelTools.name: delete_documents
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Remove specific document(s) from the active project using their IDs. Accepts both chunk IDs and source doc IDs (e.g. the doc_id returned by ingest_text) — source IDs are automatically expanded to all their chunks before deletion.

docIds.description: List of document IDs to delete

languageModelTools.name: list_knowledge
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all indexed sources in the active project with chunk counts.

languageModelTools.name: update_settings
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Change Axon settings for this session (not saved to disk). Supports: top_k, similarity_threshold, hybrid_search, rerank, hyde, multi_query, step_back, query_decompose, compress_context, raptor, graph_rag, graph_rag_mode, graph_rag_community, graph_rag_relations, graph_rag_ner_backend, graph_rag_depth, graph_rag_budget, graph_rag_relation_budget, code_graph, code_graph_bridge, sentence_window, sentence_window_size, crag_lite, discussion_fallback, cite.

rerank.description: Enable cross-encoder reranking of retrieved chunks

multi_query.description: Enable multi-query retrieval (3 rephrased queries merged)

step_back.description: Enable step-back prompting (abstract query before retrieval)

query_decompose.description: Enable query decomposition into atomic sub-questions

compress_context.description: Enable LLM context compression before generation

raptor.description: Enable RAPTOR hierarchical summary nodes during retrieval

graph_rag.description: Enable GraphRAG entity-graph expansion during retrieval

sentence_window.description: Enable sentence-window retrieval (expands chunks with surrounding sentences)

sentence_window_size.description: Number of surrounding sentences to include on each side (default 2)

crag_lite.description: Enable CRAG-lite corrective retrieval (re-queries on low-confidence chunks)

code_graph.description: Enable code graph retrieval (uses code symbol graph for code-related queries)

graph_rag_mode.description: GraphRAG query mode: "local" (entity neighbourhood), "global" (community summaries), or "hybrid"

discussion_fallback.description: Allow general-knowledge fallback when no relevant chunks are found (default false for tools)

cite.description: Include inline source citations in generated answers

graph_rag_community.description: Enable community detection on the entity graph (Louvain clustering)

graph_rag_relations.description: Extract subject-relation-object triples during ingest (builds graph edges)

graph_rag_ner_backend.description: NER backend for entity extraction: "llm" (default) or "gliner" (fast local model)

graph_rag_depth.description: GraphRAG extraction depth: "light" (entities only), "standard" (entities + relations), or "deep" (+ claims)

graph_rag_budget.description: Max entity-graph hops to expand per query (default 3)

graph_rag_relation_budget.description: Max chunks to extract relations from per ingest batch (default 30)

code_graph_bridge.description: Link code symbols to prose chunks that mention them

languageModelTools.name: share_project
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generate an HMAC share key that lets another user access one of your projects. Returns a share_string to send to the grantee out-of-band (email, Slack, etc.) and a key_id for future revocation.

project.description: Name of the project to share (must exist in your AxonStore)

grantee.description: OS username of the recipient on the shared filesystem

languageModelTools.name: redeem_share
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Redeem a share string received from another user to create a mount descriptor in your mounts/ directory. After redemption the shared project appears in your project list as mounts/{owner}_{project} and can be queried.

share_string.description: The base64-encoded share string received from the project owner

languageModelTools.name: revoke_share
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Revoke a previously issued share key by key_id. The grantee's mount will be removed on their next access. Use list_shares to find the key_id to revoke.

key_id.description: The key_id of the share to revoke (e.g. sk_a1b2c3d4). Get it from list_shares.

languageModelTools.name: list_shares
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all active shares for the current user. Returns 'sharing' (projects this user has shared with others, with revocation status) and 'shared' (projects others have shared with this user, with mount names). Use to audit access or troubleshoot missing shared projects.

languageModelTools.name: init_store
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Change the store base path (e.g. to move data to a shared drive). By default (persist=false) the change is in-memory only and config.yaml is NOT modified. Pass persist=true to write the new path to config.yaml so it survives restarts.

base_path.description: Absolute path to the directory where AxonStore/ will be created (e.g. '/data' creates '/data/AxonStore/<username>/')

persist.description: Set true to write the new store path to config.yaml so it survives server restarts. Defaults to false — omit this for test or temporary AxonStore sessions to avoid permanently modifying the user config.

languageModelTools.name: get_store_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Check whether the AxonStore has been initialised. Returns store metadata (path, version, creation date) when ready, or {"initialized": false} on a fresh install. Call this on startup before any other Axon tool.

languageModelTools.name: show_graph
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Open the Axon graph panel for a query. Shows the answer, source citations, and the entity/relationship graph. Use when the user asks to 'show the graph', 'visualise', or 'see connections' for a topic.

query.description: The question or topic to visualise

languageModelTools.name: ingest_image
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Describe an image file (PNG, JPG, JPEG, BMP, TIFF, WEBP) using Copilot's multimodal vision model, then ingest the description into the Axon knowledge base. Requires a Copilot model with image support (e.g. GPT-4o, Claude 3.x). The image path must be an absolute local path accessible to VS Code.

imagePath.description: Absolute path to the image file (PNG, JPG, JPEG, BMP, TIFF, or WEBP)

project.description: Target project namespace. Omit to use the active project.

languageModelTools.name: refresh_ingest
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Re-ingest tracked files whose content has changed since the last ingest. Returns a job_id immediately — poll with axon_getIngestStatus until status is 'completed', then check reingested/skipped/missing/errors counts. Use after editing source files to keep the knowledge base current without re-ingesting everything.

languageModelTools.name: get_stale_docs
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Return documents that have not been re-ingested within N calendar days. Use to identify which parts of the knowledge base are outdated and may need refreshing. Pair with refresh_ingest to update stale sources.

days.description: Age threshold in days. Documents older than this are returned. Default: 7.

languageModelTools.name: clear_knowledge
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Clear the entire vector store and BM25 index for the current project. This is destructive and cannot be undone  -- all ingested documents for the active project are removed. Use only when a full re-ingest is intended.

languageModelTools.name: graph_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Return the current GraphRAG community build status: community_build_in_progress (bool) and community_summary_count (int). Use to check whether community summaries are ready before running a global-mode query.

languageModelTools.name: get_current_settings
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read the current active Axon configuration: retrieval flags (top_k, rerank, hybrid, hyde, etc.), RAG mode settings (sentence_window, crag_lite, graph_rag_mode), and generation settings (llm_provider, llm_model, temperature). Use when the user asks what settings are active or wants to inspect before changing them.

languageModelTools.name: graph_finalize
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Trigger a community rebuild on the Axon knowledge graph. Rebuilds community summaries used for global-mode GraphRAG queries. Returns the number of community summaries generated. Run after ingesting a large batch of documents or when community_summary_count is 0.

languageModelTools.name: ingest_texts
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Ingest multiple text documents into Axon in one batch call. Use when you have more than one text to add. Input: docs (array of {text, metadata} objects), project (optional).

docs.description: Array of {text, metadata} objects to ingest

languageModelTools.name: list_sessions
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all saved chat sessions for the active project. Returns session IDs and metadata.

languageModelTools.name: get_session
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retrieve a specific chat session by its ID. Input: session_id (string, required).

languageModelTools.name: graph_data
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Return the full entity/relation knowledge-graph as JSON (nodes and links). Use to inspect or export the graph. Returns empty arrays when no graph has been built.

languageModelTools.name: get_active_leases
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Return active write-lease counts for all projects. Operator tool — use to check whether it is safe to put a project into maintenance mode (wait for active_leases to reach 0 first).

languageModelTools.name: axonConfigValidate
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validates the Axon config.yaml file and returns a list of issues (errors, warnings, and info).

languageModelTools.name: axonConfigSet
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sets one or more Axon config fields using dot-notation keys (e.g. chunk.strategy, llm.model, rag.top_k).

changes.description: Map of dot-notation key to new value, e.g. {"chunk.strategy": "markdown", "rag.top_k": 15}

persist.description: Whether to save changes to config.yaml on disk (default: true)

languageModelTools.name: seal_project
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Encrypt every content file in a project at rest (one-shot). Idempotent — re-sealing an already-sealed project is a no-op. Requires the sealed-store to be unlocked first (security_unlock).

project_name.description: Name of the open project to seal. Must exist.

migration_mode.description: Reserved for future variants; only 'in_place' is implemented.

languageModelTools.name: extend_share
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Renew a share key's expiry, or clear it (ttl_days=null). Pairs with share_project(ttl_days=...) to keep an active share alive on demand.

key_id.description: The key ID of the share to extend (from list_shares).

ttl_days.description: New time-to-live in days from now. null clears the expiry entirely.

languageModelTools.name: store_whoami
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Show current user identity and AxonStore path. Returns username, store_path, user_dir, and active_project.

axon.apiBase.description: Base URL of the Axon REST API server.

axon.apiKey.description: Optional API key for Axon (matches RAG_API_KEY on the server). Set this in your User settings (not Workspace) to avoid committing secrets to source control.

axon.topK.description: Number of knowledge base chunks to retrieve per query.

axon.autoStart.description: Automatically start the Axon API server when VS Code opens (Linux/macOS).

axon.graphSynthesis.description: When showing the knowledge graph, also call POST /query to synthesise an answer. Disable this if your LLM is slow or unavailable — the graph still renders without it.

axon.pythonPath.description: Path to the Python interpreter that has Axon installed. Leave empty for auto-detection: the extension checks ~/.axon/.python_path (written by the axon CLI on first run), then the pipx venv, then .venv/venv in the workspace, then system Python.

axon.useCopilotLlm.description: Use GitHub Copilot's Language Model as the primary LLM provider for Axon. This enables RAPTOR and GraphRAG ingestion without needing a local Ollama instance.

axon.ingestBase.description: Restrict ingestion to paths under this directory (sets RAG_INGEST_BASE on the server). Leave empty to allow any local path (default). Set to a specific directory to limit where the server can read files from.

axon.storeBase.description: Base path for AxonStore (e.g. /data or ~/axon-data). When set, all projects are stored under <storeBase>/AxonStore/<os-username>/. Leave empty for single-user mode (~/.axon/projects).

axon.showGraphOnQuery.description: Automatically open the Axon graph panel after every search or query. Off by default — use show_graph or the 'Show Graph for Query' command to open the graph on demand.
